sections.label: Section 295
sections.original: μετʼ ἀνδρὸς οἰκεῖν βαρβάρου πρὸς πλουσίαν
sections.translation: and live with a barbarian man , seated at his sumptuous table ? But whenever a husband she hates lives with a woman , her own body is also hateful to her . It is best to die ; how could this not be well done ? To hang oneself , high in the air , is unseemly ,

sections.label: Section 300
sections.original: κἀν τοῖσι δούλοις δυσπρεπὲς νομίζεται ·
sections.translation: even slaves consider it undignified ; but to stab oneself is noble and good , and the moment of rending life from the body is short . For I have come to such a depth of misery ; other women have good fortune from their beauty ,

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: γυναῖκες , ἡμᾶς δʼ αὐτὸ τοῦτʼ ἀπώλεσεν .
sections.translation: but the same thing has destroyed me .

sections.label: Section 306
sections.original: Ἑλένη , τὸν ἐλθόνθʼ , ὅστις ἐστὶν ὁ ξένος ,
sections.translation: Helen , do not suppose that stranger who came here , whoever he was , has spoken the whole truth .

sections.label: Section 308
sections.original: καὶ μὴν σαφῶς γʼ ἔλεξʼ ὀλωλέναι πόσιν .
sections.translation: And yet he said very clearly that my husband was dead .

sections.label: Section 309
sections.original: πόλλʼ ἂν γένοιτο καὶ διὰ ψευδῶν ἔπη .
sections.translation: Many words might be said in falsehood also .

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: καὶ τἄμπαλίν γε τῶνδʼ ἀληθείᾳ σαφῆ .
sections.translation: And the opposite of falsehood is clear in its truth .

sections.label: Section 311
sections.original: ἐς ξυμφορὰν γὰρ ἀντὶ τἀγαθοῦ φέρῃ .
sections.translation: You are carried towards misfortune instead of what is good .

sections.label: Section 312
sections.original: φόβος γὰρ ἐς τὸ δεῖμα περιβαλών μʼ ἄγει .
sections.translation: Yes , for terror has embraced me and leads me to the thing I fear .

sections.label: Section 313
sections.original: πῶς δʼ εὐμενείας τοισίδʼ ἐν δόμοις ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: How much goodwill is there in this house for you ?

sections.label: Section 314
sections.original: πάντες φίλοι μοι πλὴν ὁ θηρεύων γάμους .
sections.translation: All are my friends , except the one who hunts me in marriage .

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: οἶσθʼ οὖν ὃ δρᾶσον ; μνήματος λιποῦσʼ ἕδραν —
sections.translation: Then do you know what you should do ? Leave your seat at the monument —

sections.label: Section 316
sections.original: ἐς ποῖον ἕρπεις μῦθον ἢ παραίνεσιν ;
sections.translation: What sort of word of advice are you coming to ?

sections.label: Section 317
sections.original: ἐλθοῦσʼ ἐς οἴκους , ἣ τὰ πάντʼ ἐπίσταται ,
sections.translation: Go inside and question the daughter of the sea-nymph , Theonoe , who knows all things , about your husband , whether he is still alive , or has left the light of day ;

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: εἴτʼ ἐκλέλοιπε φέγγος · ἐκμαθοῦσα δʼ εὖ
sections.translation: and when you know for certain , rejoice or be full of mourning , according to your fortune . Before you know anything correctly , what good would it do you to grieve ? But be persuaded by me ; leave this tomb and join the girl ;

sections.label: Section 325
sections.original: ὅθενπερ εἴσῃ πάντα τἀληθῆ μαθεῖν
sections.translation: when you have someone in this house from whom you can learn the whole truth , why do you look further ? And I myself want to go in and ask about the prophecies of the maiden with you ; for , truly , women ought to help each other .

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: φίλαι , λόγους ἐδεξάμαν ·
sections.translation: Dear friends , I welcome your advice . Come in , come into the house , to learn within about my struggles .

sections.label: Section 334
sections.original: θέλουσαν οὐ μόλις καλεῖς .
sections.translation: You are calling on one who is wholly willing .

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: ἰὼ μέλεος ἁμέρα .
sections.translation: Oh , what an unhappy day ! What tearful word shall I hear , unhappy as I am ?

sections.label: Section 338
sections.original: μὴ πρόμαντις ἀλγέων
sections.translation: Do not be a prophetess of sorrow , dear friend , anticipating lamentation .

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: τί μοι πόσις μέλεος ἔτλα ;
sections.translation: What has my poor husband suffered ? Does he see the light and the sun ’ s chariot and the paths of the stars ? Or does he have a lasting fate

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: τὰν χρόνιον ἔχει τύχαν ;
sections.translation: among the dead beneath the earth ?

sections.label: Section 346
sections.original: ἐς τὸ φέρτερον τίθει
sections.translation: Take a brighter view of the future , whatever will happen .

sections.label: Section 348
sections.original: σὲ γὰρ ἐκάλεσα , σὲ δὲ κατόμοσα ,
sections.translation: For I call on you , I swear to you , Eurotas green with watery reeds ,

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: Εὐρώταν , θανόντος
sections.translation: if this rumor of my husband ’ s death is true — and what was obscure in those words ?— I will stretch a deadly noose about my neck , or drive inward a murderous thrust of slaughter that gushes from the throat ,

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: λαιμορρύτου σφαγᾶς
sections.translation: a contest of the blade through my flesh , as a sacrifice to the three goddesses and to the son of Priam , who once sat on the hollows of Ida , near the ox-stalls .

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: ἄλλοσʼ ἀποτροπὰ κακῶν
sections.translation: May sorrow be turned aside elsewhere , and may your lot be fortunate !

sections.label: Section 362
sections.original: ἰὼ Τροία τάλαινα ,
sections.translation: Oh , unhappy Troy ! Through deeds not done by yourself , you are ruined , and have suffered pitiably ; for the gift that Kypris gave me has caused much blood

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: πολὺ δὲ δάκρυον · ἄχεά τʼ ἄχεσι ,
sections.translation: and many tears ; it has added grief to grief and tear to tear , sorrows . . . . Mothers have lost their children and virgin sisters of the slain have cut off their hair by the swollen tide of Phrygian Skamandros .

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: βοὰν βοὰν δʼ Ἑλλὰς
sections.translation: And Hellas has cried aloud , aloud , and broken forth in wailing , beating her head , and drenching her soft-skinned cheek with the bloody strokes of her nails .

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: ὦ μάκαρ Ἀρκαδίᾳ ποτὲ παρθένε Καλλιστοῖ , Διὸς
sections.translation: O maiden Kallisto , blessed once in Arcadia , who climbed into the bed of Zeus on four paws , how much happier was your lot than my mother ’ s , you who in the form of a shaggy-limbed beast — the bearing of a lioness with your fierce eye — changed your burden of sorrow ;

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: ἐξαλλάξασʼ ἄχθεα λύπης ·
sections.translation: and also the one whom Artemis once drove from her chorus , as a deer with horns of gold , the Titan girl , daughter of Merops , because of her loveliness ; but the beauty of my body has destroyed the Dardanian towers , it has destroyed them and

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: ὀλομένους τʼ Ἀχαιούς .
sections.translation: the lost Achaeans .

sections.label: Section 386
sections.original: ὦ τὰς τεθρίππους Οἰνομάῳ Πῖσαν κάτα
sections.translation: O Pelops , who once held that chariot-race contest with Oinomaos over Pisa , if only , when you were persuaded to make a banquet for the gods , you had left your life then , inside the gods ,

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: πρὶν τὸν ἐμὸν Ἀτρέα πατέρα γεννῆσαί ποτε ,
sections.translation: before you ever begot my father , Atreus , to whom were born , from his marriage with Airope , Agamemnon and myself , Menelaos , a famous pair ; for I believe that I carried a mighty army — and I say this not in boast — in ships to Troy ,

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: τύραννος οὐδὲν πρὸς βίαν στρατηλατῶν ,
sections.translation: no tyrant commanding any troops by force , but leading the young men of Hellas by voluntary consent . And some of these can be counted no longer alive , others as having a joyful escape from the sea , bringing home again names thought to be of the dead .

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: ἐγὼ δʼ ἐπʼ οἶδμα πόντιον γλαυκῆς ἁλὸς
sections.translation: But I wander miserably over the swelling waves of the gray ocean , ever since I sacked the towers of Ilion ; and although I long to come home , I am not thought worthy by the gods to achieve this . I have sailed to Libya ’ s deserts and all its inhospitable landing-places ;

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: πέπλευκα πάσας · χὥταν ἐγγὺς ὦ πάτρας ,
sections.translation: and whenever I draw near my native land , the blast drives me back again , and no favoring wind has ever entered my sails to let me come home .

sections.label: Section 408
sections.original: καὶ νῦν τάλας ναυαγὸς ἀπολέσας φίλους
sections.translation: And now I am cast up on this shore , a miserable shipwrecked sailor who has lost his friends ; and my ship is

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: πολλοὺς ἀριθμοὺς ἄγνυται ναυαγίων .
sections.translation: broken into many pieces against the rocks . But out of its cleverly-wrought fastenings the keel was left , on which I made my difficult escape by an unexpected chance , and also Helen with me , whom I dragged away from Troy . But I do not know the name of this country

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα · ὄχλον γὰρ ἐσπεσεῖν ᾐσχυνόμην
sections.translation: and its people ; for I was embarrassed to burst into a crowd and make inquiries , and so I concealed these shabby clothes , in shame over my misfortune . For whenever a man of high degree is badly off , he falls into an unaccustomed state which is worse than that of one who has long been unfortunate .

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: χρεία δὲ τείρει μʼ · οὔτε γὰρ σῖτος πάρα
sections.translation: But poverty is wearing me away ; for I have neither food , nor clothes around my body ; one can compare what I am wearing to rags cast out of the ship . The sea snatched away all the robes I once wore , splendid clothes and ornaments . Deep in a cave

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original: κρύψας γυναῖκα τὴν κακῶν πάντων ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: I hid the woman who caused all my troubles , and have come here , after compelling those of my friends who survived to guard my wife . I have come alone , seeking help for those friends there , if I may find it somehow after careful search .

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original: ἰδὼν δὲ δῶμα περιφερὲς θριγκοῖς τόδε
sections.translation: When I saw this home , surrounded by walls , and the majestic gateways of some prosperous man , I came near ; sailors can hope to get something from wealthy homes ; but from those who have no livelihood — they could not help us , even if they wanted to .

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: ὠή · τίς ἂν πυλωρὸς ἐκ δόμων μόλοι ,
sections.translation: Hello ! Is there some gatekeeper who might come from the house and announce my troubles within ?

sections.label: Section 437
sections.original: τίς πρὸς πύλαισιν ; οὐκ ἀπαλλάξῃ δόμων
sections.translation: Who ’ s at the door ? Get away from the house and don ’ t annoy my master by standing at the court-yard gate ! Or else you will die because you are

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: Ἕλλην πεφυκώς , οἷσιν οὐκ ἐπιστροφαί .
sections.translation: a Hellene , and they have no business here .

sections.label: Section 441
sections.original: ὦ γραῖα , ταὐτὰ ταῦτʼ ἔπη κἄλλως λέγειν
sections.translation: My good woman , you can say these same words in a different tone , for I shall be persuaded ; but let go your angry speech .

sections.label: Section 443
sections.original: ἄπελθʼ · ἐμοὶ γὰρ τοῦτο πρόσκειται , ξένε ,
sections.translation: Go away ! Stranger , my orders are to let no Hellene come near this house .

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: ἆ · μὴ προσείλει χεῖρα μηδʼ ὤθει βίᾳ .
sections.translation: Ah ! Do not push me , or thrust me away by force .

sections.label: Section 446
sections.original: πείθῃ γὰρ οὐδὲν ὧν λέγω , σὺ δʼ αἴτιος .
sections.translation: You are to blame , for not heeding what I say .

sections.label: Section 447
sections.original: ἄγγειλον εἴσω δεσπόταισι τοῖσι σοῖς ⋯
sections.translation: Announce to your master inside —

sections.label: Section 448
sections.original: πικρῶς ἄρʼ οἶμαί γʼ ἀγγελεῖν τοὺς σοὺς λόγους .
sections.translation: I think someone would be sorry , if I announced your words .

sections.label: Section 449
sections.original: ναυαγὸς ἥκω ξένος , ἀσύλητον γένος .
sections.translation: I come as a shipwrecked man and a guest ; such people are safe from violence .

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: οἶκον πρὸς ἄλλον νύν τινʼ ἀντὶ τοῦδʼ ἴθι .
sections.translation: Well , go to some other house instead of this one .

sections.label: Section 451
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλʼ ἔσω πάρειμι · καὶ σύ μοι πιθοῦ .
sections.translation: No ; I am going inside . You listen to me .

sections.label: Section 452
sections.original: ὀχληρὸς ἴσθʼ ὤν · καὶ τάχʼ ὠσθήσῃ βίᾳ .
sections.translation: Know that you ’ re only causing trouble ; and soon you ’ ll be thrown out by force .

sections.label: Section 453
sections.original: αἰαῖ · τὰ κλεινὰ ποῦ ʼστί μοι στρατεύματα ;
sections.translation: Alas ! Where are those glorious armies of mine ?

sections.label: Section 454
sections.original: οὐκοῦν ἐκεῖ που σεμνὸς ἦσθʼ , οὐκ ἐνθάδε .
sections.translation: Perhaps you were grand somewhere , but not here .

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original: ὦ δαῖμον , ὡς ἀνάξιʼ ἠτιμώμεθα .
sections.translation: O my fortune , how we have been unworthily dishonored .
